project.name: Refund Triage Assistant Plan
project.purpose: Plan a support-agent assistant that drafts refund recommendations from tickets and the internal policy page, escalates ambiguity, and excludes refund approval, customer contact, and runtimes.

non_goals.title: Do not issue refunds, approve refunds, or contact customers.
Do not replace supervisor judgment when policy is ambiguous or ticket facts conflict with policy.
Do not add an execution runtime, shell adapter, Codex adapter, task queue, or live support integration.

capabilities.title: Draft refund recommendations for support agents.
Escalate ambiguous or conflicting refund policy cases.
Maintain synchronized human docs and machine contract records from conversation.

requirements.title: Draft recommendations only; never approve, issue, or initiate refunds.
Cite ticket facts and the internal refund policy section used.
Escalate policy ambiguity or ticket-policy conflict to a supervisor.
Escalation language must not invent policy or customer commitments.
Model-authored planning turns must update docs/plan/** and .ni/contract.json together.
Readiness, lock, and prompt compilation must be delegated to ni status, ni end, and ni run rather than model judgment.

decisions.title: Treat the internal refund policy page as the authoritative eligibility source.
Use transcript fixtures as the first evaluation method for refund recommendation behavior.
Use human-team as the first downstream prompt target after lock.
Final production owner for maintaining the refund policy page.

risks.title: The assistant may imply refund approval authority that belongs to humans or payment systems.
risks.mitigation: Require recommendation language, agent review, and transcript checks for approval or customer-contact wording.

risks.title: Ticket context may contain private customer data.
risks.mitigation: Use only ticket facts needed for triage and keep generated handoffs inside the support-agent workflow.

risks.title: A stale or unclear policy page could create incorrect refund recommendations.
risks.mitigation: Cite policy, escalate conflicts or unclear sections, and keep policy ownership visibly deferred.

risks.title: Docs and contract records may drift if the model updates only one source.
risks.mitigation: Name changed docs and affected IDs each authoring turn, then run ni status.

evaluations.title: Refund recommendation transcript fixtures.
evaluations.method: Review eligible and ineligible refund transcript fixtures for correct recommendation, cited facts, cited policy source, and absence of refund issuance or customer-contact language.

evaluations.title: Ambiguous policy escalation transcript fixture.
evaluations.method: Review a policy ambiguity or ticket-policy conflict fixture and verify supervisor escalation with no invented policy and a clear next action for the support agent.

evaluations.title: Conversation authoring audit fixture.
evaluations.method: Check the transcript, docs/plan sections, and .ni/contract.json for matching CAP, REQ, EVAL, RISK, ART, NG, DEC, and OQ records after the model-authored turn.

open_questions.title: Which support dashboard will eventually display the recommendation draft?
